title: Relevance Realization Meets Dynamical Systems Theory
essence: Building on distinctions between representational theories and direct relevance realization, Vervaeke introduces dynamical systems thinking via Darwin's theory of evolution. Just as biological fitness isn't an essence to be detected but a process constantly evolving through selection and variation, cognitive relevance realization must be understood not as discovering pre-existing relevance but as an ongoing self-organizing process. Using the framework of opponent processing and logistical norms (efficiency vs. resiliency), he argues that the brain's sensory-motor loop undergoes continuous optimization of cognitive-interactional fitness—a non-homuncular, multi-scale process grounded in the bioeconomy. This foundations the scientific explanation of relevance realization as a natural phenomenon continuous with biological evolution.
keyClaims: Relevance cannot be theoretically explained because it lacks systematic import—the homogeneity, stability, and intrinsic properties required for scientific induction.
Biological fitness has no essential design; it constantly evolves through opponent processing in dynamic coupling with the environment.
Relevance realization functions as 'cognitive interactional fitedness'—the ongoing evolution of how an organism fits problems to its capacities and environment.
The sensory-motor loop is regulated by a virtual engine of opponent processing between efficiency and resiliency.
Logistical norms (not semantic truth norms) govern the bioeconomy's distribution of metabolic resources, attention, and processing power.

concepts.name: relevance realization
concepts.summary: Established as a non-representational, self-organizing process distinct from relevance-detection; understood as evolution of cognitive-interactional fitness.

concepts.name: dynamical systems
concepts.summary: Framework for understanding relevance realization as a self-organizing, multi-scale process analogous to evolutionary dynamics.

concepts.name: cognitive interactional fitedness
concepts.summary: The continuous evolution of an organism's cognitive and behavioral coupling to its environment through virtual engines of opponent processing.

concepts.name: opponent processing
concepts.summary: Systems with opposed goals but integrated function (e.g., sympathetic and parasympathetic); enables dynamic optimization.

concepts.name: logistical norms
concepts.summary: Efficiency and resiliency as governing constraints on bioeconomic resource allocation, distinct from semantic truth norms.

concepts.name: bioeconomy
concepts.summary: The body as a self-organizing system distributing metabolic and cognitive resources toward maintaining its own organization.

concepts.name: virtual engine
concepts.summary: A self-organizing dynamical system (like selection-variation in evolution) that optimizes a system without requiring external intelligence.

concepts.name: efficiency
concepts.summary: Logistical norm favoring resource conservation through generalization; tendency to compress and integrate.

concepts.name: resiliency
concepts.summary: Logistical norm enabling adaptation to unexpected change through maintaining slack; tendency to particularize and differentiate.

people.name: Charles Darwin
people.summary: Theory of evolution via natural selection provides the foundational analogy for understanding relevance realization as non-essentialist, process-driven optimization.

people.name: John Stuart Mill
people.summary: On systematic import and inductive generalization; demonstrates why relevance cannot be made scientific.

people.name: Willard Quine
people.summary: On essence and inductive generalization; distinguishes deductive from inductive essences.

people.name: Ludwig Wittgenstein
people.summary: On family resemblance and categories without essences; critiques essentialism for natural language concepts.

people.name: Markus Brede
people.summary: Mathematical work showing optimization between efficiency and resiliency in networks.

crossRefs.context: Earlier discussion of Darwin, dynamical systems, and Aristotle; foundations being drawn upon.
Previous episode on relevance realization; this extends the theoretical machinery.
Next episode will expand on embodied, embedded relevance realization as dynamical-developmental.

quotableMoments.quote: There is no essence to fitedness. If you try and come up with a theory of how organisms have their 'design' in terms of trying to determine or derive it from the essence of design, you are doomed because it doesn't exist.
quotableMoments.context: On why evolutionary fitness cannot be essentialized, foundational analogy for relevance realization.

quotableMoments.quote: Relevance Realization is not something for which we can have a scientific theory... there is no essence to relevance; nothing is essentially relevant.
quotableMoments.context: The core argument against scientific theory of relevance as distinct from theory of relevance realization.

quotableMoments.quote: Fitedness and the evolution of fitedness are the same thing.
quotableMoments.context: Explaining that there is no pre-existing fitness to discover, only ongoing evolutionary processes.
